ogTitle: Hazelwood South vs Healesville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hazelwood South and Healesville.
heading: Hazelwood South vs Healesville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hazelwood South, Victoria and Healesville, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Healesville has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Hazelwood South (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hazelwood South or Healesville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hazelwood South has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Healesville scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hazelwood South scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Healesville scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hazelwood South or Healesville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Healesville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
